</svg>

            </div>

            <div class="nav-title" id="nav-title"></div>

            <div class="nav-actions">
                <button id="copy-btn" title="Copy Markdown">
                    <svg id="copy-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2" ry="2"></rect>
                        <path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"></path>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="copy-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
                <button id="open-btn" title="Open in Native App">
                    <svg id="open-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" 
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"></path>
                        <polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"></polyline>
                        <line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"></line>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="open-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
            </div>
        </nav>

        <div class="content-wrapper" id="content-wrapper">
            <div id="app">
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 36px; width: 40%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 95%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 90%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 80%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="fade-overlay" id="fade-overlay"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="toggle-container" id="toggle-container">
            <button id="toggle-btn">
                <span id="toggle-text">Show More</span>
                <svg id="toggle-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                    stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" style="transform: rotate(0deg);">
                    <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"></polyline>
                </svg>
            </button>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        const appDiv = document.getElementById('app');
        const INIT_ID = 1;
        let isExpanded = false; // START COLLAPSED BY DEFAULT!

        let rawMarkdownText = "";
        let currentFilePath = null;
        const navTitle = document.getElementById('nav-title');
        const copyBtn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
        const copyIcon = document.getElementById('copy-icon');
        const copyText = document.getElementById('copy-text');
        const openBtn = document.getElementById('open-btn');
        const openText = document.getElementById('open-text');

        function fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(text) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const textArea = document.createElement("textarea");
                textArea.value = text;
                textArea.style.top = "0";
                textArea.style.left = "0";
                textArea.style.position = "fixed";
                textArea.style.opacity = "0";

                document.body.appendChild(textArea);
                textArea.focus();
                textArea.select();

                try {
                    const successful = document.execCommand('copy');
                    if (successful) {
                        resolve();
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error("execCommand returned false"));
                    }
                } catch (err) {
                    reject(err);
                } finally {
                    document.body.removeChild(textArea);
                }
            });
        }

        copyBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
            try {
                if (navigator.clipboard && window.isSecureContext) {
                    try {
                        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(rawMarkdownText);
                    } catch (e) {
                        await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                    }
                } else {
                    await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                }

                const originalSvg = copyIcon.innerHTML;
                copyIcon.innerHTML = `<polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"></polyline>`;

                copyText.textContent = "Copied!";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.style.color = "#10b981";

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyIcon.innerHTML = originalSvg;
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.style.color = "";
                }, 2000);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error('Failed to copy text: ', err);
                copyText.textContent = "Failed";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.title = err.toString();

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.title = "Copy Markdown";
                }, 3000);
            }
        });

        openBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
            if (!currentFilePath) return;

            const reqId = Date.now();

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                id: reqId,
                method: "tools/call",
                params: {
                    name: "open_local_file",
                    arguments: {
                        file_path: currentFilePath
                    }
                }
            }, "*");

            openText.textContent = "Opening...";
            openText.style.display = "inline";
            
            setTimeout(() => {
                openText.style.display = "none";
                openText.textContent = "";
            }, 2000);
        });

        function escapeHtml(unsafe) {
            return (unsafe || "").toString()
                .replace(/&/g, "&")
                .replace(/</g, "<")
                .replace(/>/g, ">")
                .replace(/"/g, """)
                .replace(/'/g, "'");
        }

        function showError(title, message, details = null) {
            let html = `
            <div class="alert">
                <div class="alert-title">
                    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><line x1="12" y1="8" x2="12" y2="12"></line><line x1="12" y1="16" x2="12.01" y2="16"></line></svg>
                    ${escapeHtml(title)}
                </div>
                <div class="alert-body">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
            `;
            if (details) {
                html += `<div class="alert-details">${escapeHtml(details)}</div>`;
            }
            html += `</div>`;
            appDiv.innerHTML = html;
        }

        function checkCollapsible() {
            const wrapper = document.getElementById('content-wrapper');
            const toggleContainer = document.getElementById('toggle-container');
            const fadeOverlay = document.getElementById('fade-overlay');
            const toggleIcon = document.getElementById('toggle-icon');
            const toggleText = document.getElementById('toggle-text');

            // 1. If no markdown, we are showing the skeleton. Do not collapse!
            if (!rawMarkdownText) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                triggerResize();
                return;
            }

            // 2. Data is loaded
            const realHeight = appDiv.scrollHeight;
            const COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD = 300;
            const COLLAPSED_HEIGHT = 150;
            const EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT = 500;

            if (realHeight > COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                if (isExpanded) {
                    // Open view! Scroll inside max 500px window
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'auto';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show Less';
                } else {
                    // Collapsed View
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = COLLAPSED_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'hidden';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'block';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show More';
                }
            } else {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
            }

            triggerResize();
        }

        document.getElementById('toggle-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
            isExpanded = !isExpanded;
            checkCollapsible();
        });

        function triggerResize() {
            // Measure the exact unconstrained height of the outer card
            const card = document.querySelector('.adeu-card');
            const containerChrome = 34; // 16px padding top/bottom + 2px borders

            const requestedHeight = card.offsetHeight + containerChrome;

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                params: { height: Math.ceil(requestedHeight), width: 600 }
            }, "*");
        }

        window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
            try {
                if (!event.data || event.data.jsonrpc !== "2.0") return;
                const msg = event.data;

                if (msg.id === INIT_ID) {
                    window.parent.postMessage({
                        jsonrpc: "2.0",
                        method: "ui/notifications/initialized",
                        params: {}
                    }, "*");
                    return;
                }

                if (msg.method === "ui/notifications/tool-result") {
                    if (window.__MARKED_ERROR || typeof marked === 'undefined') {
                        showError("Missing Dependency", "The Markdown renderer failed to load.", window.__MARKED_ERROR || "marked is undefined.");
                        return;
                    }

                    const result = msg.params;

                    // If the state is pending, or no markdown is returned, completely hide the UI
                    if (!result || !result.structuredContent || result.structuredContent.status === "pending" || !result.structuredContent.markdown) {
                        document.documentElement.style.display = 'none';
                        window.parent.postMessage({
                            jsonrpc: "2.0",
                            method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                            params: { height: 0, width: 0 }
                        }, "*");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Render Markdown
                    rawMarkdownText = result.structuredContent.markdown;
                    if (result.structuredContent.file_path) {
                        currentFilePath = result.structuredContent.file_path;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'flex';
                    } else {
                        currentFilePath = null;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'none';
                    }
                    appDiv.innerHTML = marked.parse(rawMarkdownText);
                    copyBtn.style.display = 'flex';

                    if (result.structuredContent.title) {
                        navTitle.textContent = result.structuredContent.title;
                        navTitle.title = result.structuredContent.title;
                    }
                }
            } catch (error) {
                showError("Rendering Engine Error", "An unexpected error occurred.", error.toString() + "\n" + error.stack);
            }
        });

        const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
            checkCollapsible();
        });
        observer.observe(appDiv);

        window.parent.postMessage({
            jsonrpc: "2.0",
            id: INIT_ID,
            method: "ui/initialize",
            params: {
                appInfo: { name: "Adeu Markdown UI", version: "1.1.0" },
                appCapabilities: {},
                protocolVersion: "2025-11-21"
            }
        }, "*");
    </script>
</body>

</html>

content.text:
> **File Path:** `/root/Documents/Oscar GC/Commercial/S32-810479 Sprint 30 â Pemberton RFQ pack + redline c05/03-pricing-schedule.docx`

# Pricing Schedule

Pemberton Engineering Ltd.Â â Indirect MRO Framework â 3-Year Term

**Document 03 of 7 â to be completed by the Bidder and returned with bid**

**Pemberton ref:** PEL/PROC/2026-RFQ-073 **Issued:** 11 May 2026

This Pricing Schedule forms part of the Master Supply Agreement (Document 02). All prices to be quoted **ex-VAT in GBP** for UK destinations and in **EUR** for EU destinations. Bidders must complete every line; âPOAâ or âTBCâ responses will result in disqualification.

# 1. Category Scope

The framework covers the following indirect MRO categories:

Cat | Category | Indicative Annual Spend (ÂŁ)
A | Cutting tools and abrasives | 4,200,000
B | Industrial fasteners | 2,800,000
C | Lubricants and process fluids | 3,400,000
D | PPE and workwear | 1,900,000
E | Electrical components and motors | 3,100,000
F | Pneumatics and hydraulics | 2,000,000
G | Capital spares (nominated SKU list) | 1,000,000
 | **Total indicative annual spend** | **18,400,000**

Spend figures are based on the twelve months ending 31 March 2026 and are indicative only. No commitment is made to any minimum volume.

# 2. Ceiling Prices

For each SKU in Appendix A (the SKU master list â 1,247 SKUs), bidders must quote a unit ceiling price. The ceiling price represents the maximum the Bidder may charge during the first twelve (12) months of the Term, irrespective of actual market price movements.

# 3. Rebate Ladder

Bidders shall propose a rebate structure on the following ladder. Rebates accrue on aggregated group spend (across all UK and EU sites) per calendar quarter and are paid in arrears within thirty (30) days of quarter end by credit note.

Tier | Quarterly aggregate spend (ÂŁ) | Min. rebate %
1 | Up to 750,000 | 0.5%
2 | 750,001 â 1,500,000 | 1.5%
3 | 1,500,001 â 2,500,000 | 2.5%
4 | Above 2,500,000 | 4.0%

Bidders may propose higher rebate percentages or additional tiers in their bid response.

# 4. Volume Discount Triggers

Quarterly volume discounts above the rebate ladder apply automatically on the following category-level triggers:

* Category A spend exceeds ÂŁ1.2m in a quarter: additional 1.0% on Category A

* Category C spend exceeds ÂŁ1.0m in a quarter: additional 0.75% on Category C

* Category E spend exceeds ÂŁ900k in a quarter: additional 1.0% on Category E

# 5. Currency and Payment

5.1 UK invoices in GBP. EU invoices in EUR at the local siteâs billing entity.

5.2 Foreign exchange risk is borne by the Supplier. No FX-related price adjustments are permitted within the first twelve (12) months.

5.3 Payment terms per MSA clause 6 (90 days from receipt of valid invoice).

# 6. Open-Book Costing for Top-50 SKUs

For the fifty (50) highest-spend SKUs as identified by Buyer from time to time, Supplier shall provide open-book costing comprising landed cost, Supplier margin, and any rebates received from upstream manufacturers. Buyer reserves the right to require margin compression where Supplier margin exceeds twelve percent (12%) on any open-book SKU.

# 7. Most-Favoured-Customer

Supplier represents that, for substantially comparable Goods supplied on substantially comparable terms, the prices quoted under this Pricing Schedule are no less favourable than those offered to any other customer of Supplier. Supplier shall promptly notify Buyer of any improvement in pricing offered to a third party and shall extend such improvement to Buyer.

# 8. Pricing Schedule Return Format

Bidders shall return Appendix A as an Excel workbook with the columns set out in the template. PDF returns will not be accepted. Bidders must also provide:

* A written justification for any SKU where the quoted ceiling price exceeds Buyerâs internal benchmark by more than twenty percent (20%) (Buyer will share benchmark deltas during the clarification window with shortlisted bidders);

* An explanation of methodology for any FX, freight, or duty assumptions baked into landed cost;

* A statement of any sustainability premia included in pricing (e.g.Â low-carbon steel, recycled-content fasteners).

# Appendix A â SKU Master List (Extract)

SKU | Description | UoM | Indicative annual qty | Bid ceiling price (ÂŁ)
A-0001 | HSS twist drill bit, 6mm | each | 12,400 | __________
A-0002 | Carbide insert, CCMT 09T308 | each | 38,200 | __________
A-0103 | Diamond abrasive disc, 230mm | each | 6,900 | __________
B-0044 | M12 hex bolt, A2-70, 60mm | each | 184,000 | __________
C-0017 | ISO VG 68 hydraulic oil | 208L drum | 412 | __________
D-0202 | Cut-resistant glove level D, size L | pair | 24,800 | __________
E-0091 | 3-phase induction motor 5.5kW IE3 | each | 92 | __________
F-0028 | Pneumatic cylinder ISO 15552 80x200 | each | 148 | __________
G-0007 | Bearing 6308-2RS | each | 2,420 | __________

_[Full SKU master list of 1,247 lines provided as separate Excel workbook on portal â extract above is illustrative.]_

---
## Footnotes

contents.text:
<!-- FILE: src/adeu/templates/markdown_ui.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Adeu Markdown UI</title>
    <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
    <link
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Jost:wght@400;500;600&family=Lora:ital,wght@0,600;1,600&display=swap"
        rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
        :root {
            --brand-sand: #e8c0a1;
            --brand-sand-light: #f2d9c7;
            --brand-sand-dark: #d4a882;
            --brand-mint: #e6f3f0;
            --brand-mint-dark: #c9e4de;
            --brand-navy: #1a2a2c;
            --brand-navy-light: #2a3a3c;
            --brand-mauve: #dacad2;
            --neutral-50: #fafafa;
            --neutral-100: #f5f5f5;
            --neutral-200: #e5e5e5;
            --neutral-300: #d9d9d9;
            --neutral-600: #555969;
            --neutral-800: #1e2028;
            --neutral-900: #171717;
            --font-lora: 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
            --font-jost: 'Jost', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
            --bg-body: var(--neutral-50);
            --bg-card: #ffffff;
            --border-card: var(--neutral-200);
            --text-main: var(--neutral-600);
            --text-heading: var(--brand-navy);
            --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.15);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            :root {
                --bg-body: var(--neutral-900);
                --bg-card: var(--brand-navy);
                --border-card: var(--brand-navy-light);
                --text-main: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
                --text-heading: #ffffff;
                --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.1);
                --neutral-100: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        html,
        body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box;
            background-color: transparent;
        }

        body {
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            color: var(--text-main);
            line-height: 1.6;
            -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
            padding: 16px;
        }

        .adeu-card {
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            border: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 12px;
            overflow: hidden;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }

        .navbar {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            padding: 16px 32px;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: space-between;
            position: relative;
        }

        .logo-container {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            flex: 1;
        }

        .nav-title {
            position: absolute;
            left: 50%;
            transform: translateX(-50%);
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-size: 1.1rem;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
            max-width: 40%;
            text-align: center;
        }

        .nav-actions {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: flex-end;
            flex: 1;
        }

        #copy-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #copy-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        #open-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #open-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #copy-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
            #open-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        .logo-container svg {
            max-width: 80px;
            height: auto;
        }

        .logo-container svg path,
        .logo-container svg rect,
        .logo-container svg polygon,
        .logo-container svg circle {
            transition: fill 0.2s ease;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {

            .logo-container svg path,
            .logo-container svg rect,
            .logo-container svg polygon,
            .logo-container svg circle,
            .logo-container svg {
                fill: #ffffff !important;
            }
        }

        .content-wrapper {
            position: relative;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar {
            width: 6px;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
            background: transparent;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
            background-color: var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        #app {
            padding: 32px;
            display: flow-root;
        }

        .fade-overlay {
            position: absolute;
            bottom: 0;
            left: 0;
            right: 0;
            height: 80px;
            background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0), var(--bg-card));
            pointer-events: none;
            display: none;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .fade-overlay {
                background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(26, 42, 44, 0), var(--bg-card));
            }
        }

        .toggle-container {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            display: none;
            justify-content: center;
            align-items: center;
            padding: 12px;
            border-top: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
        }

        #toggle-btn {
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.95rem;
            cursor: pointer;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            padding: 8px 16px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #toggle-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #toggle-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        #toggle-icon {
            transition: transform 0.3s ease;
        }

        h1,
        h2,
        h3,
        h4 {
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-weight: 600;
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            margin-top: 1.5em;
            margin-bottom: 0.5em;
        }

        h1 {
            font-size: 2.25rem;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            padding-bottom: 0.3em;
            margin-top: 0;
        }

        h2 {
            font-size: 1.75rem;
        }

        h3 {
            font-size: 1.25rem;
        }

        a {
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            text-decoration: none;
            border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;
            transition: border 0.2s ease;
        }

        a:hover {
            border-bottom-color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
        }

        strong {
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
        }

        ul,
        ol {
            padding-left: 1.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
        }

        li {
            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        }

        blockquote {
            border-left: 4px solid var(--brand-sand);
            padding: 12px 20px;
            margin: 24px 0;
            color: var(--text-main);
            background: var(--bg-blockquote);
            border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;
            font-style: italic;
        }

        code {
            font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            background: var(--neutral-100);
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            padding: 0.2em 0.4em;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        pre code {
            display: block;
            padding: 16px;
            background: var(--neutral-900);
            color: #e5e5e5;
            border-radius: 8px;
            overflow-x: auto;
        }

        .skeleton {
            background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--neutral-100) 25%, var(--border-card) 50%, var(--neutral-100) 75%);
            background-size: 200% 100%;
            animation: skeleton 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        @keyframes skeleton {
            0% {
                background-position: 200% 0;
            }

            100% {
                background-position: -200% 0;
            }
        }

        .alert {
            padding: 16px;
            border-radius: 8px;
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            gap: 8px;
            background-color: #fef2f2;
            border: 1px solid #fecaca;
            color: #991b1b;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .alert {
                background-color: rgba(127, 29, 29, 0.2);
                border-color: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.3);
                color: #fca5a5;
            }
        }

        .alert-title {
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 1.1em;
            margin: 0;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 8px;
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
        }

        .alert-body {
            margin: 0;
            font-size: 0.95em;
        }

        .alert-details {
            background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
            padding: 12px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            white-space: pre-wrap;
            word-break: break-all;
            margin-top: 8px;
            border: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
        }
    </style>

            <div class="logo-container">

</svg>

            </div>

            <div class="nav-title" id="nav-title"></div>

            <div class="nav-actions">
                <button id="copy-btn" title="Copy Markdown">
                    <svg id="copy-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2" ry="2"></rect>
                        <path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"></path>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="copy-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
                <button id="open-btn" title="Open in Native App">
                    <svg id="open-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" 
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"></path>
                        <polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"></polyline>
                        <line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"></line>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="open-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
            </div>
        </nav>

        <div class="content-wrapper" id="content-wrapper">
            <div id="app">
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 36px; width: 40%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 95%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 90%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 80%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="fade-overlay" id="fade-overlay"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="toggle-container" id="toggle-container">
            <button id="toggle-btn">
                <span id="toggle-text">Show More</span>
                <svg id="toggle-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                    stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" style="transform: rotate(0deg);">
                    <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"></polyline>
                </svg>
            </button>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        const appDiv = document.getElementById('app');
        const INIT_ID = 1;
        let isExpanded = false; // START COLLAPSED BY DEFAULT!

        let rawMarkdownText = "";
        let currentFilePath = null;
        const navTitle = document.getElementById('nav-title');
        const copyBtn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
        const copyIcon = document.getElementById('copy-icon');
        const copyText = document.getElementById('copy-text');
        const openBtn = document.getElementById('open-btn');
        const openText = document.getElementById('open-text');

        function fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(text) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const textArea = document.createElement("textarea");
                textArea.value = text;
                textArea.style.top = "0";
                textArea.style.left = "0";
                textArea.style.position = "fixed";
                textArea.style.opacity = "0";

                document.body.appendChild(textArea);
                textArea.focus();
                textArea.select();

                try {
                    const successful = document.execCommand('copy');
                    if (successful) {
                        resolve();
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error("execCommand returned false"));
                    }
                } catch (err) {
                    reject(err);
                } finally {
                    document.body.removeChild(textArea);
                }
            });
        }

        copyBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
            try {
                if (navigator.clipboard && window.isSecureContext) {
                    try {
                        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(rawMarkdownText);
                    } catch (e) {
                        await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                    }
                } else {
                    await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                }

                const originalSvg = copyIcon.innerHTML;
                copyIcon.innerHTML = `<polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"></polyline>`;

                copyText.textContent = "Copied!";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.style.color = "#10b981";

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyIcon.innerHTML = originalSvg;
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.style.color = "";
                }, 2000);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error('Failed to copy text: ', err);
                copyText.textContent = "Failed";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.title = err.toString();

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.title = "Copy Markdown";
                }, 3000);
            }
        });

        openBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
            if (!currentFilePath) return;

            const reqId = Date.now();

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                id: reqId,
                method: "tools/call",
                params: {
                    name: "open_local_file",
                    arguments: {
                        file_path: currentFilePath
                    }
                }
            }, "*");

            openText.textContent = "Opening...";
            openText.style.display = "inline";
            
            setTimeout(() => {
                openText.style.display = "none";
                openText.textContent = "";
            }, 2000);
        });

        function escapeHtml(unsafe) {
            return (unsafe || "").toString()
                .replace(/&/g, "&")
                .replace(/</g, "<")
                .replace(/>/g, ">")
                .replace(/"/g, """)
                .replace(/'/g, "'");
        }

        function showError(title, message, details = null) {
            let html = `
            <div class="alert">
                <div class="alert-title">
                    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><line x1="12" y1="8" x2="12" y2="12"></line><line x1="12" y1="16" x2="12.01" y2="16"></line></svg>
                    ${escapeHtml(title)}
                </div>
                <div class="alert-body">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
            `;
            if (details) {
                html += `<div class="alert-details">${escapeHtml(details)}</div>`;
            }
            html += `</div>`;
            appDiv.innerHTML = html;
        }

        function checkCollapsible() {
            const wrapper = document.getElementById('content-wrapper');
            const toggleContainer = document.getElementById('toggle-container');
            const fadeOverlay = document.getElementById('fade-overlay');
            const toggleIcon = document.getElementById('toggle-icon');
            const toggleText = document.getElementById('toggle-text');

            // 1. If no markdown, we are showing the skeleton. Do not collapse!
            if (!rawMarkdownText) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                triggerResize();
                return;
            }

            // 2. Data is loaded
            const realHeight = appDiv.scrollHeight;
            const COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD = 300;
            const COLLAPSED_HEIGHT = 150;
            const EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT = 500;

            if (realHeight > COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                if (isExpanded) {
                    // Open view! Scroll inside max 500px window
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'auto';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show Less';
                } else {
                    // Collapsed View
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = COLLAPSED_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'hidden';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'block';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show More';
                }
            } else {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
            }

            triggerResize();
        }

        document.getElementById('toggle-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
            isExpanded = !isExpanded;
            checkCollapsible();
        });

        function triggerResize() {
            // Measure the exact unconstrained height of the outer card
            const card = document.querySelector('.adeu-card');
            const containerChrome = 34; // 16px padding top/bottom + 2px borders

            const requestedHeight = card.offsetHeight + containerChrome;

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                params: { height: Math.ceil(requestedHeight), width: 600 }
            }, "*");
        }

        window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
            try {
                if (!event.data || event.data.jsonrpc !== "2.0") return;
                const msg = event.data;

                if (msg.id === INIT_ID) {
                    window.parent.postMessage({
                        jsonrpc: "2.0",
                        method: "ui/notifications/initialized",
                        params: {}
                    }, "*");
                    return;
                }

                if (msg.method === "ui/notifications/tool-result") {
                    if (window.__MARKED_ERROR || typeof marked === 'undefined') {
                        showError("Missing Dependency", "The Markdown renderer failed to load.", window.__MARKED_ERROR || "marked is undefined.");
                        return;
                    }

                    const result = msg.params;

                    // If the state is pending, or no markdown is returned, completely hide the UI
                    if (!result || !result.structuredContent || result.structuredContent.status === "pending" || !result.structuredContent.markdown) {
                        document.documentElement.style.display = 'none';
                        window.parent.postMessage({
                            jsonrpc: "2.0",
                            method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                            params: { height: 0, width: 0 }
                        }, "*");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Render Markdown
                    rawMarkdownText = result.structuredContent.markdown;
                    if (result.structuredContent.file_path) {
                        currentFilePath = result.structuredContent.file_path;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'flex';
                    } else {
                        currentFilePath = null;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'none';
                    }
                    appDiv.innerHTML = marked.parse(rawMarkdownText);
                    copyBtn.style.display = 'flex';

                    if (result.structuredContent.title) {
                        navTitle.textContent = result.structuredContent.title;
                        navTitle.title = result.structuredContent.title;
                    }
                }
            } catch (error) {
                showError("Rendering Engine Error", "An unexpected error occurred.", error.toString() + "\n" + error.stack);
            }
        });

        const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
            checkCollapsible();
        });
        observer.observe(appDiv);

        window.parent.postMessage({
            jsonrpc: "2.0",
            id: INIT_ID,
            method: "ui/initialize",
            params: {
                appInfo: { name: "Adeu Markdown UI", version: "1.1.0" },
                appCapabilities: {},
                protocolVersion: "2025-11-21"
            }
        }, "*");
    </script>
</body>

</html>

blocks.text:
Excellent. Now I have the full picture. Let me synthesize the key findings into a strategic memo for you.

---

## **PEMBERTON RFQ â COMMERCIAL POSITION MEMO**

**Buyer:** Pemberton Engineering Ltd. (UK-based manufacturer, 3+ UK manufacturing sites, 2 EU sites)  
**Scope:** 3-year non-exclusive indirect MRO supply framework, ÂŁ18.4m annual indicative spend (no minimum commitment)  
**Key Issue:** Pemberton's GTCs and MSA form a buyer-favorable stack with material commercial and legal asymmetries.

---

### **TOP-3 WALK-AWAY ISSUES**

#### **1. Unilateral Buyer Termination for Convenience (90-day wind-down, capped 5% cost recovery)**

**The risk:** MSA Â§18.1 gives Pemberton the right to terminate at any time with 30 days' notice. Our early-termination liability is capped at 5% of work-in-progress value onlyâno recovery for stranded tooling, inventory write-off, personnel ramp-down, fixed-cost absorption, or customer-notification costs. Over a ÂŁ18.4m/year spend, a sudden termination mid-contract could strand ÂŁ2â3m in indirect costs we cannot recover.

**Why walk-away?** This is a pseudo-rolling 30-day contract masquerading as a 3-year framework. Combined with the 90-day payment terms (clause 6.2), Pemberton can accumulate ÂŁ4.5m+ in payables, terminate, and owe us nothing beyond 5% of incomplete work. Unilateral termination for convenience without genuine wind-down cost recovery is commercially unworkable on a ÂŁ18m framework.

**Pushback required:** Reciprocal termination for convenience, or trigger-based compensation for supplier-incurred stranded costs (inventory, tooling, certified personnel, demobilisation). Minimum 90-day cure period for material breach.

---

#### **2. Uncapped Indemnity + Broad Scope Drift (MSA Â§10, GTCs Remedies clause)**

**The risk:** Indemnity in MSA Â§10 is completely uncapped and survives termination. It covers breach of contract, negligence, defect, IP infringement, *and* "any breach of applicable law." The GTCs reinforce this: remedies for warranty breach include repair, replacement, refund, *and* damages "without prejudice to any other right or remedy" including the right to terminate (GTCs Â§8.1). Taken together, Pemberton can claim consequential damages for any alleged regulatory breach, product liability, IP issue, or operational failureâwithout cap and without preclusion of termination.

**Why walk-away?** The scope of the indemnity is incoherent (mixing breach, tort, defect, regulatory, and IP into one uncapped bucket). No rational supplier can price for unlimited liability for regulatory compliance, IP indemnity, and third-party product claims on a ÂŁ18m/year indirect MRO supply. The interaction with GTCs remedies Â§8.1 (which explicitly carves out remedy exclusions) creates a backdoor around the liability cap in MSA Â§11.1. This is a $100m+ tail risk on a commodity supply contract.

**Pushback required:** Decompose indemnities by head (defect/warranty, IP infringement, regulatory breach). Cap IP indemnity at 12 months' fees. Exclude consequential damages universally. Require causation and mitigation. Permit insurance-backed indemnification where feasible (e.g., product liability indemnity via Supplier's insurance certificate).

---

#### **3. All Foreground IP Vests Absolutely in Buyer + Perpetual Background IP Licence (MSA Â§9)**

**The risk:** Â§9.2 assigns *all* Foreground IP "arising in connection with this Agreement" to Pemberton absolutely, including any modifications, improvements, derivatives, or joint materials. Â§9.4 grants Pemberton a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide, sublicensable licence to any Background IP "embedded in or required to exploit" the Goods. On an MRO supply contract, "embedded" Background IP means our proprietary sourcing methodologies, supplier relationships, logistics optimizations, and even internal cost models become Pemberton's property.

**Why walk-away?** For a commodity MRO supply, there is minimal *joint* Foreground IP; most "improvements" are Buyer-driven customization or our internal cost-reduction measures. Assigning Buyer all derivatives, modifications, and improvements means we cannot replicate learnings across other customers. The perpetual, sublicensable licence on embedded Background IP means Pemberton can resell our sourcing expertise or hand it to a rival. This clause strips our competitive moat on MRO procurement efficiency.

**Pushback required:** Exclude Background IP from assignment. Limit Foreground IP assignment to work product *specifically created for Buyer* per documented specification. Retain ownership of process improvements, sourcing methodologies, and generic tools. License back jointly-developed IP to Supplier on a royalty-free basis for use with non-competing customers.

---

### **TOP-5 HIGH-PRIORITY PUSH-BACK ITEMS**

#### **4. 90-Day Payment Terms (MSA Â§6.2)**

**Commercial impact:** Combined with ÂŁ18.4m annual spend, 90-day terms mean Pemberton holds ~ÂŁ4.6m of our cash for 3 months in arrears. GTCs allow Pemberton to withhold disputed portions indefinitely (GTCs Â§6.3). The statutory late-payment interest (8% + BoE base) is a token remedy on a framework this large.

**Market position:** 60 days is market standard for UK MRO supply; 90 days is aggressive. Should be 60 days, or 90 days with a material-spend trigger tier (e.g., 60 days if quarterly spend > ÂŁ2m, 90 days otherwise).

**Pushback:** 60 days net on all invoices, or 90 days only if quarterly spend in preceding quarter < ÂŁ1.5m.

---

#### **5. Most-Favoured-Customer (MFC) Clause + Open-Book Costing on Top 50 SKUs (Pricing Schedule Â§7, Â§6)**

**Commercial impact:** Pricing Schedule Â§7 binds us to MFC: we must offer Pemberton any better pricing we give *any other customer*. Combined with open-book costing on the top-50 SKUs (Pricing Schedule Â§6), Pemberton can trigger price reductions on our highest-volume SKUs by threatening to audit our margins on third-party comparables. Buying Group data sharing (common in MRO procurement) means Pemberton will benchmark against peer buying groups. The 12% margin cap (Â§6) is punitive on fast-moving spares.

**Market position:** MFC on commodity supply is routine. Open-book costing on a subset of SKUs is also market practice. *But the combination*âespecially without a defined customer cohort and without carve-outs for volume, service, or geographyâcreates a continuous ratchet down.

**Pushback:** Narrow MFC to "customers ordering > ÂŁ10m annually on materially identical terms" (i.e., exclude smaller or special-arrangement customers). Limit open-book audit to annual basis, not ad-hoc. Carve out SKUs where we carry inventory risk or provide non-standard service. Allow margin compression above 12% only if material cost changes drive it (indexed to commodity indices).

---

#### **6. Audit Rights â Scope, Notice, Duration (MSA Â§16, Compliance Annex Audit Cooperation)**

**Risk exposure:** MSA Â§16.1 allows audits on 5 business days' notice. GTCs Â§6.3 extends audit to books, records, premises, systems, *personnel*, and *sub-suppliers*. Compliance Annex adds audits for Modern Slavery due diligence and sanctions screening. Audit rights survive termination 6 years (Â§16.3). Auditors can be Buyer's employees or third-party auditors (cost borne by Supplier). No limit on audit frequency.

**Market standard:** 10â15 business days' notice, on-site audits max twice per year, auditors limited to Big 4 or equivalent, Buyer bears audit cost, 3-4 year survival post-term.

**Pushback:** 10 business days' notice; max 2 audits per calendar year (except post-breach); auditors must be Big 4 or Buyer's internal staff (not competitive intelligence firms); Buyer bears cost; survival limited to 4 years post-term; audits limited to records directly supporting Supplier's performance, not general operational audit.

---

#### **7. Data Processing Annex Incorporation by Reference + Unilateral Update Right (MSA Â§15, Compliance Annex Data Protection)**

**Risk exposure:** MSA Â§15.1 incorporates a Data Processing Annex "on its current terms from time to time"âmeaning Pemberton can unilaterally update DPA terms post-signature without our consent. Compliance Annex Data Protection section requires GDPR + UK GDPR compliance and obliges us to notify Pemberton of personal data breaches within 24 hours. The "from time to time" language means Pemberton could tighten data-retention rules, add new processors, or impose sub-processor audit rights without amendment.

**Why problematic?** GDPR/UK GDPR compliance is standard. *Unilateral amendment* to DPA terms is not. This creates an indefinite compliance tail where Pemberton can escalate data obligations mid-contract.

**Pushback:** DPA must be fixed at a known version on execution date. Changes require written amendment signed by both parties. Limit personal data processing to purposes necessary to perform the supply (no marketing, no third-party transfer without explicit consent).

---

#### **8. Termination for Change of Control to Competitor (MSA Â§18.2(iii))**

**Risk exposure:** Either party can terminate if the other undergoes "change of control to a competitor of Buyer." But "competitor" is undefined. In practice, this means Pemberton has a unilateral veto on any M&A we undergo that they deem competitiveâeven horizontal acquisitions or roll-ups that don't directly compete with Pemberton's end products. If we are acquired by a larger MRO distributor, Pemberton can terminate without cause or cost.

**Market position:** Change-of-control termination rights are routine. *Undefined "competitor" is not.* Most deals define competitor by specific product category or geography, and tie the termination right to material conflict of interest.

**Pushback:** Define "competitor" narrowly: "a person or entity that (i) derives >25% of revenue from the design, manufacture, or supply of goods or services that directly compete with Buyer's finished products, AND (ii) would have access to confidential Buyer information as a result of the Change of Control." Require Buyer to notify Supplier of any concern within 30 days of announcement; permit Supplier 90 days to remedy (via divestment, firewalls, or sub-contracting).

---

#### **9. Material Breach Trigger: 3 Consecutive Months Below OTIF Target (MSA Â§7.3, SLA)**

**Risk exposure:** Failure to meet On-Time-In-Full (OTIF) targets for 3 consecutive months triggers material breach of the MSA. SLA Measurement shows OTIF targets vary by category (88â95%), with SLA credits and penalties that accumulate. The SLA credit table shows that if we miss OTIF in any rolling 3-month window, Pemberton can invoke material breach and terminate without cure period (if non-remediable) or with only 30 days to fix (if remediable).

**Why problematic?** OTIF is supply-chain dependent (upstream suppliers, logistics partners, port delays, etc.). A single bad month from an upstream supplier can trigger a cascade toward material breach within 90 days. The 30-day cure window on a 3-month SLA miss is unrealistic for supply-chain recovery. Pemberton can also apply for termination termination for convenience (Â§18.1) at the same time, leaving us no real remedy.

**Market position:** OTIF SLAs are standard. Material breach on 3 consecutive misses is punitive; market norm is 2 consecutive months or cumulative 50% miss in a rolling 6-month window, with 60-day cure. The current wording means one bad quarter = material breach = termination.

**Pushback:** Reframe material breach trigger to "cumulative OTIF miss â„ 25% in any rolling 6-month period, uncured for 60 days after notice." Exclude force-majeure events (port strikes, logistics failures beyond Supplier control). Allow Supplier to propose remediation plan in lieu of termination (e.g., dual-source, inventory prepositioning, expedited freight).

---

#### **10. ESG Attestation + SBTi Net-Zero Commitment (Compliance Annex)**

**Risk exposure:** Compliance Annex requires annual ESG attestation signed at director level, including Modern Slavery statement, Bribery Act procedures, sanctions screening, *and* Scope 1/2/3 GHG emissions reporting under GHG Protocol, with a published Science-Based Target initiative (SBTi) Net-Zero target by Year 3 of the Term (i.e., by December 2029). Non-compliance triggers material breach (Annex, Failure to Comply section).

**Why problematic?** SBTi Net-Zero validation requires 18â24 months of work with an external verifier and board sign-off. Scope 3 emissions (our supply chain's emissions) are often outside our direct control and require supplier engagement at scale. Requiring a published SBTi target by Dec 2029 on a contract commencing Jan 2027 gives us ~3 yearsâfeasible only if we have significant GHG infrastructure and board commitment already. Non-compliance = material breach.

**Market position:** ESG attestation and GHG reporting are increasingly routine. SBTi Net-Zero *validation* (not commitment) is market-leading practice for Tier 1 suppliers to mega-procurers. Requiring *published* and *validated* SBTi by Year 3 on a new contract is aggressive and may be unachievable if we lack current certifications.

**Pushback:** Decouple ESG attestation (annual, director-signed) from GHG target validation. Require reporting of Scope 1/2 GHG and baseline Scope 3 by Year 1. Target = "committed to Science-Based Target by Year 5" (2032) with interim milestones (Year 2: submit target for validation; Year 3: achieve validation). Make failure to report = breach; failure to achieve validated target by Year 5 = material breach only if we have failed to make good-faith progress.

---

### **ACCEPTABLE / NICE-TO-HAVE ITEMS**

- **Pricing Schedule rebate ladder & volume triggers:** Market-standard. Propose enhancing Tier 4 to 5% if feasible; negotiate to tier rebates by category (higher for A, C, E where we can drive volume) and lower for G (capital spares, where volume is constrained).
- **Ceiling prices (first 12 months) + CPI+2.5% thereafter:** Acceptable. First-year firm pricing is standard; 2.5% annual cap is reasonable if rebate ladder incentivizes high volume (which compresses our unit margin).
- **Insurance requirements (Â§12):** ÂŁ10m product liability, ÂŁ5m professional indemnity, statutory employer's liability. Standard; achievable via broker.
- **Governing law (English law, Manchester courts):** Acceptable for UK operations. Manchester (vs. London) slightly increases dispute costs but is reasonable.
- **Order of precedence (GTCs > MSA > SLA > Pricing Schedule > PO):** GTCs prevailing is buyer-standard in procurement. Flag the precedence for team awareness but it's not a deal-breaker if MSA holds key protective clauses (liability cap, indemnity scope, termination rights).

---

### **STRATEGIC FRAMING FOR THE COMMERCIAL TEAM**

Pemberton is a sophisticated buyer on a large framework. They've used their standard procurement GTCs (which are buyer-favorable) as the top-layer document, subordinating the MSA. This is a classic procurement move: load the GTCs with indemnities and remedy clauses, then let the MSA contain secondary detail that conflicts with GTCs.

**Your negotiation posture should be:**

1. **Use the walk-away items (1, 2, 3) as anchors.** Signal that reciprocal termination for convenience, scoped/capped indemnities, and retained IP are non-negotiable. You're not asking for buyer-favorable terms; you're asking for mutual risk-sharing on a 3-year framework worth ÂŁ18m+.

2. **Trade item 5 (payment terms) and item 6 (MFC + open-book)** against item 1 (termination). If Pemberton won't reciprocate termination for convenience, push hard on 60-day payment and narrow MFC to defined cohort.

3. **Item 9 (OTIF material breach) is high-leverage.** Pemberton needs reliable supply; a supplier that lives in fear of termination on OTIF miss #3 will hedge with inventory and dual-sourcing, which kills margin. Reframe as "mutually beneficial: we propose OTIF targets AND a remediation protocol so you get supply continuity and we get fair breach tolerance."

4. **Item 10 (ESG/SBTi) is achievable with a timeline reset.** Don't refuse; just decouple GHG reporting from validated SBTi target achievement and push Year 5 validation (not Year 3).

---

### **NEXT STEPS**

1. **Bid decision:** Can your commercial team absorb 90-day payment terms if we win the volume? If yes, and if you can execute on ESG reporting and cyber-security requirements, the RFQ is viable *if* we redline the walk-aways.

2. **Redline priority:** Load the redlined MSA with fixes to Â§Â§9, 10, 11, 15, 16, 18 before addressing the GTCs. The GTCs are Buyer's house terms; realistically Pemberton won't shift those. The MSA is where we have leeway.

3. **Clarification window:** This RFQ has a "clarification window" (per invitation letter). Use it to ask (a) whether Buyer will consider reciprocal termination for convenience, (b) whether "competitor" in Â§18.2(iii) can be defined, and (c) whether SBTi target can be pushed to Year 5. This signals seriousness and gives Buyer a chance to move before we submit the redline.

4. **Escalation:** Walk-aways 1 and 2 should go to GC and CFO before bid submission, especially if pricing is competitive but tight. Unilateral termination + uncapped indemnity can turn a margin-neutral deal into a loss.

---

Let me know if you want me to draft the redlined MSA now, or if you'd like to discuss any of these points further. The clock is on the bid return dateâ15 June per the invitation letterâso we have ~3 weeks.
What's our exposure if their GTCs (Document 05) override the MSA on indemnities? Worst case, what are we signing up to?
I need to read the full GTCs carefully to map the precedence and scope interplay. Let me pull the complete text and trace how the indemnity language cascades across both documents.
